### get_db

>  get_db (ns:dict=None)

``` python
db = get_db(globals())
dlg = db.t.dialog.fetchone()
dlg
```

### find_var

>  find_var (var:str)

*Search for var in all frames of the call stack*

``` python
a = 1
find_var('a')
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/dialoghelper/blob/main/dialoghelper/core.py#L44"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_dialog_id

>  find_dialog_id ()

\*Get the dialog id by searching the call stack for \_\_dialog_id.\*

``` python
__dialog_id = dlg.id
```

``` python
find_dialog_id()
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/dialoghelper/blob/main/dialoghelper/core.py#L49"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_msgs

>  find_msgs (pattern:str, limit:int=10)

*Find messages in a specific dialog that contain the given pattern. To
refer to a message found later, use its `sid` field.*

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>pattern</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>Text to search for</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>limit</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>10</td>
<td>Limit number of returned items</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
found = find_msgs('to the')
found[0]
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/dialoghelper/blob/main/dialoghelper/core.py#L60"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_msg_id

>  find_msg_id ()

\*Get the message id by searching the call stack for \_\_dialog_id.\*

``` python
__msg_id = found[0].sid
```

``` python
find_msg_id()
```

### msg_idx

>  msg_idx ()

*Get index of current message in dialog.*

``` python
ids,idx = msg_idx()
idx
```

### read_msg

>  read_msg (n:int=-1, relative:bool=True)

*Get the message indexed in the current dialog.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>n</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>-1</td>
<td>Message index (if relative, +ve is downwards)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>relative</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Is <code>n</code> relative to current message (True) or absolute
(False)?</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
# Previous message relative to current
read_msg(-1)
```

``` python
# Last message in dialog
read_msg(-1, relative=False)
```

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/dialoghelper/blob/main/dialoghelper/core.py#L106"

### add_msg

>  add_msg (content:str, msg_type:str='note', output:str='',
>               placement:str='add_after', sid:str=None,
>               input_tokens:int|None=0, output_tokens:int|None=0,
>               time_run:str|None='', is_exported:int|None=0,
>               skipped:int|None=0, did:int|None=None, i_collapsed:int|None=0,
>               o_collapsed:int|None=0, header_collapsed:int|None=0,
>               pinned:int|None=0)

*Add/update a message to the queue to show after code execution
completes. Be sure to pass a `sid` (stable id) not a `mid` (which is
used only for sorting, and can change).*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>content</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>content of the message (i.e the message prompt, code, or note
text)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>msg_type</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>note</td>
<td>message type, can be ‘code’, ‘note’, or ‘prompt’</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>output</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>for prompts/code, initial output</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>placement</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>add_after</td>
<td>can be ‘add_after’, ‘add_before’, ‘update’, ‘at_start’,
‘at_end’</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sid</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>sid of message that placement is relative to (if None, uses current
message)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>input_tokens</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>output_tokens</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>time_run</td>
<td>str | None</td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>is_exported</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skipped</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>did</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>None</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>i_collapsed</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>o_collapsed</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>header_collapsed</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>pinned</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### update_msg

>  update_msg (msg:Optional[Dict]=None, sid:str=None, content:str=None,
>                  msg_type:str='note', output:str='',
>                  placement:str='add_after', input_tokens:int|None=0,
>                  output_tokens:int|None=0, time_run:str|None='',
>                  is_exported:int|None=0, skipped:int|None=0,
>                  did:int|None=None, i_collapsed:int|None=0,
>                  o_collapsed:int|None=0, header_collapsed:int|None=0,
>                  pinned:int|None=0)

*Update an existing message. Provide either `msg` OR field key/values to
update. Use `content` param to update contents. Be sure to pass a `sid`
(stable id) not a `mid` (which is used only for sorting, and can
change).*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>

<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>msg</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Dictionary of field keys/values to update</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sid</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>sid of message that placement is relative to (if None, uses current
message)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>content</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>content of the message (i.e the message prompt, code, or note
text)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>msg_type</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>note</td>
<td>message type, can be ‘code’, ‘note’, or ‘prompt’</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>output</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>for prompts/code, initial output</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>placement</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>add_after</td>
<td>can be ‘add_after’, ‘add_before’, ‘update’, ‘at_start’,
‘at_end’</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>input_tokens</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>output_tokens</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>time_run</td>
<td>str | None</td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>is_exported</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>

<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>did</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>None</td>
<td></td>

<td>i_collapsed</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>o_collapsed</td>
<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>

<td>int | None</td>
<td>0</td>
<td></td>
</tr>

### load_gist

>  load_gist (gist_id:str)

*Retrieve a gist*

``` python
gistid = 'jph00/e7cfd4ded593e8ef6217e78a0131960c'
gist = load_gist(gistid)
gist.html_url
```

    'https://gist.github.com/jph00/e7cfd4ded593e8ef6217e78a0131960c'

### gist_file

>  gist_file (gist_id:str)

*Get the first file from a gist*

``` python
gfile = gist_file(gistid)
print(gfile.content)
```

    testfoo='testbar'

### import_gist

>  import_gist (gist_id:str, mod_name:str=None, add_global:bool=True)

*Import gist directly from string without saving to disk*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>gist_id</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>user/id or just id of gist to import as a module</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>mod_name</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>module name to create (taken from gist filename if not passed)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>add_global</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>add module to caller’s globals?</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
import_gist(gistid)
importtest.testfoo
```

    'testbar'
